claim: SoNash already operates 14 distinct memory mechanisms across 5 tiers, making it one of the most memory-mature Claude Code projects found in the research landscape.

The primary live auto-memory at ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ has diverged from the canonical-memory copy in .claude/canonical-memory/. The canonical copy is missing ~7 of 18 feedback entries and describes user expertise incorrectly.
The MCP knowledge graph server (mcp__memory) is configured and permitted but shows low active utilization. The checkpoint skill documents it but no evidence of frequent actual usage was found.
The episodic-memory plugin permission is scoped to search only. The full retrieval tool (episodic_memory_show) is not permitted, limiting the plugin to finding but not reading past conversations.
SoNash has 8,473 technical debt items in MASTER_DEBT.jsonl with a documented overwrite hazard — multiple ingestion scripts can regenerate the file, potentially losing deferred/resolved status.
The SoNash learning system has 88.5% effectiveness rate, 444 documented patterns, and 70 automated — an unusually mature behavioral reinforcement system.
STATE_SCHEMA.md documents 10 state files; the actual .claude/state/ directory contains 82 files. The schema document is significantly stale.
claude-mem (thedotmack) is the most widely-adopted Claude Code memory plugin with ~38,400 GitHub stars and a progressive 3-layer disclosure pattern claiming ~10x token savings over naive injection.

everything-claude-code (ECC) is the highest-starred Claude Code plugin found (~124,000 stars), implementing 5 memory layers from session markdown through instincts YAML to a proposed SQLite knowledge graph.

cipher (campfirein) is the only reviewed memory tool implementing reasoning trace capture ('System 2 / Reflection Memory') — storing how the agent thought, not just what happened. Its quality threshold (>=0.4) gates what gets stored.
claude-supermemory requires a $19/month Pro plan for Claude Code plugin access. The free tier does not include it. A Windows stdin bug (Issue #25) was open as of February 2026.

Anthropic's Auto Memory system (GA since v2.1.59) automatically writes session learnings to ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md, injecting the first 200 lines / 25KB at session start. It is machine-local by design.

Auto Dream (background memory consolidation) exists in the Claude Code codebase as a server-side feature flag (tengu_onyx_plover, enabled:false). The /dream command returns 'Unknown skill: dream' when invoked manually. A community implementation (dream-skill) replicates the 4-phase cycle.

The AGENTS.md standard (Linux Foundation / Agentic AI Foundation) is supported by Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Aider, Zed, Warp, RooCode. It addresses the fragmentation problem where CLAUDE.md is ignored by Cursor.
The MCP server ecosystem has 50+ memory-specific servers. PulseMCP lists 413 memory-related MCP entries. The community identified '1,000+ similar projects with zero performance benchmarks.'
Anthropic's official @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory (JSONL knowledge graph) is already configured in SoNash via .mcp.json. It runs natively on Windows with no admin access required.
mem0 reports 26% higher accuracy, 91% faster responses, and 90% token savings on LoCoMo benchmark. AWS chose it as exclusive memory provider for its Agent SDK. These benchmarks are self-reported.
Cursor removed its Memories feature in mid-2025. The community moved to .cursor/rules/ files (structurally identical to CLAUDE.md). No post-mortem explaining why it was removed has been published.

MCP tools are passive (Claude decides when to call them) while hooks are active (fire deterministically at lifecycle events). The supermemory team rebuilt from MCP-first to hooks-only for capture because they could not guarantee capture through MCP alone.
A full memory stack (Auto Memory + CLAUDE.md + MCP server) consumes 10,000-12,000 tokens before any user input. In high-accumulation scenarios this can reach 33,000-54,000 tokens.

The 'check memory at session start' instruction in CLAUDE.md triggers a documented 5-6 minute hang when the MCP server returns empty results (GitHub issue #15140, closed NOT_PLANNED). The correct pattern is hook-based injection.

The three-role model (CLAUDE.md = instructions, Auto Memory = learnings, MCP = knowledge base) emerged independently across multiple research sources as the architectural consensus for organizing Claude Code memory.

The four-file markdown pattern (activeContext.md, productContext.md, progress.md, decisionLog.md) is a de facto community standard across Cline, Roo Code, and Claude Code memory systems — independently converged upon across multiple tools.

Context rot is empirically confirmed: every model tested (Claude Opus 4, GPT-4.1, Gemini 2.5 Pro) degrades with increasing context length. Multiple distractors compound degradation non-linearly. Irrelevant injected memory actively impairs performance.

Auto Memory is explicitly machine-local per Anthropic's official documentation. Setting autoMemoryDirectory to a cloud-synced folder is not officially supported but is not blocked — a potential solo-developer workaround.

GitHub Copilot's citation-backed JIT validation (January 2026) is the most architecturally sophisticated memory approach found. Memories store code line citations; before use, citations are validated against current codebase; stale memories are discarded. It showed 3% precision increase and 7% PR merge rate increase.
Anthropic's engineering blog recommends sub-agent summaries of 1,000-2,000 tokens maximum, file naming that signals purpose, and progressive context discovery over pre-loading.

The compaction loss problem is confirmed across three independent sources: procedural knowledge generated mid-session gets compressed and nuance is lost. Only what was written to memory files before compaction fires is preserved.

MemGPT (UC Berkeley, 2023) established that file-based memory is a recognized architectural pattern (virtual context management), not a workaround. Our file-based approach maps directly to MemGPT's main context / external context model.

The five properties of true episodic memory identify a gap: most systems capture facts but not instance-specific 'when/where/why.' SoNash's MEMORY.md partially satisfies properties 1-3 but fails at property 4 (instance-specific) and partially at 5 (contextual relations).
Reflexion (NeurIPS 2023) demonstrated verbal reinforcement via text files achieves 91% pass@1 on HumanEval vs GPT-4's 80%, validating SoNash's AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md pattern as research-backed.
A-MAC (March 2026) found that 'content type prior' is the strongest single predictor of whether a memory entry should be stored — stronger than recency, semantic novelty, or utility.

AWS AgentCore's production memory extraction takes 20-40 seconds per memory, confirming that real-time memory extraction during active sessions is not feasible — memory must be built asynchronously post-session.

LongMemEval shows commercial assistants and long-context LLMs exhibit ~30% accuracy drop on memorizing information across sustained interactions. Cross-session memory persistence is unsolved even in production systems.

Only two tools explicitly confirmed Windows compatibility with path remapping for multi-machine scenarios: memoir (5 stars, 27 days old) and mcp-memory-keeper (110 stars). memoir is high-risk; mcp-memory-keeper is the only proven Windows option.
OMEGA Memory requires WSL 2 on Windows and does not run natively, despite claiming #1 LongMemEval benchmark performance (95.4%).

Native Claude Code Auto Memory does not sync cross-locale. Cross-locale sync requires: (A) cloud MCP server (~$19/mo, privacy risk), (B) self-hosted central backend via Railway, or (C) autoMemoryDirectory pointed at cloud-synced folder (unofficial, untested).

git-notes-memory stores memory in git notes that travel with the repository regardless of machine, potentially solving the cross-locale problem. This approach was identified but not deeply researched.
autoMemoryDirectory pointed at the git repo's .claude/canonical-memory/ directory is the lowest-friction cross-locale sync solution for SoNash's two-Windows-different-username scenario.

autoMemoryDirectory can only be set in settings.local.json or user-level settings, not in .claude/settings.json. This is a deliberate security restriction, not a bug, and requires per-locale manual configuration.
The .claude/canonical-memory/ directory is git-tracked and contains 20 files but is diverged from live auto-memory by at least 7 feedback entries and contains an incorrect user expertise description.

Claude Code keys auto memory to absolute filesystem paths. Two machines with different usernames but the same git repo have different memory keys and cannot access each other's auto memory without explicit intervention.
Sharing a SQLite database file via OneDrive, Dropbox, or any cloud sync service risks data corruption. SQLite explicitly documents this as unsupported. WAL mode makes the risk worse, not better.

Both claude-sync tools (tawanorg and renefichtmueller) sync ~/.claude/projects/ as-is and do not remap locale-specific path keys. They do not solve the path-key problem for different-username locales.
memoir (camgitt) claims Windows path remapping 'just works' during restore, but the mechanism is entirely undocumented. No source confirms it handles two Windows machines with different usernames.
claude-brain explicitly does not support Windows native; it requires WSL.
CCMS (miwidot) is a bash script requiring Unix or WSL. It is not viable on a no-admin Windows environment without WSL.
autoMemoryDirectory was added to Claude Code in v2.1.74 (March 12, 2026) specifically to support the use case of pointing memory to OneDrive/Dropbox/git-repo paths.
The git-notes pattern solves the path-key problem by keying memory to commit hashes instead of filesystem paths, but tooling is immature and requires Python plus a vector embedding stack.
Syncthing runs as a single portable binary on Windows with no admin required. Corporate firewalls at the work locale may block Syncthing relay servers, making home-to-work sync unreliable.
codebase-memory-mcp fills a gap that no session memory system addresses: a structural AST-based code index. Pre-built Windows amd64 binary; zero dependencies; 66 languages.

The cursor-memory-bank 4-file pattern (activeContext, productContext, progress, decisionLog) converged independently across Roo-Code, Cline, and cursor-memory-bank. It is a de facto community standard for project-scoped markdown memory.

Hook-based capture (CP-1) is the only mechanism that guarantees memory writes without requiring Claude to decide to call a store tool. Supermemory explicitly rebuilt its architecture away from MCP tools to hooks for this reason.

SoNash's existing 25-hook, 14-mechanism architecture already implements hook-based capture comprehensively. The architectural gap is not coverage but queryability: captured data is not well-exposed through a retrieval layer.
The mcp__memory knowledge graph server is configured and permitted but underused. The /checkpoint --mcp skill documents its intended use. The gap is discipline, not infrastructure.
OMEGA Memory requires WSL2 on Windows with no confirmed native Windows path. This is a hard blocker at the no-admin work locale.
claude-mem has multiple documented Windows-specific failures: AbortSignal crash, pipe mode breakage, PowerShell dependency. It is rated FRAGILE on Windows.
CLAUDE.md adherence degrades when the file exceeds 200 lines. The 135-line target in SoNash's CLAUDE.md is well-reasoned and should not be allowed to grow.

The A-MAC academic finding on 'content type prior' means the most effective memory admission gate is a whitelist of category types rather than a scoring formula. Categories: user-corrections, architecture-decisions, hook-patterns, recurring-errors.

Episodic memory (timestamped events) is the weakest layer in SoNash's current architecture. The mcp__memory server stores entities/relations but not timestamped events. MEMORY.md mixes episodic and semantic content with no temporal binding.

The MEMORY.md index to individual files pattern already partially implements progressive disclosure. Completing it requires only adding an instruction in MEMORY.md for Claude to request specific topic files. No infrastructure change needed.
Auto Dream is server-gated (feature flag tengu_onyx_plover, enabled: false) and not GA as of March 2026. The community dream-skill plugin replicates the 4-phase cycle and is available now.
OMEGA Memory and Hindsight both claim top LongMemEval rankings simultaneously. These cannot both be current. Benchmark claims from either system should be treated as indicative only.

The episodic-memory (superpowers) plugin is already configured and Windows-compatible via fnm. The show tool is not in the allow-list. Validate this existing tool before adding any new vector search infrastructure.

A type-to-decay mapping for SoNash memory files: in-progress work 7 days; session observations 30 days; bug resolutions 90 days; architecture decisions, user corrections, project constraints permanent.
Adding metadata headers (last_reviewed, type, expires) to the 39 individual memory files enables a decay-checking script without any new infrastructure. This is the lowest-cost LP-2 implementation.
The learning system has automatic consolidation (every 10 PR reviews to CODE_PATTERNS.md). Session memory has no equivalent pipeline. This is the highest-priority lifecycle gap.

Signal keyword extraction from claude-supermemory — only writing MEMORY.md entries on turns containing 'decision', 'bug', 'architecture', 'correction', 'never again' — is adoptable without adopting that system.

The '2+ projects before promotion' rule from lin-yuchen is directly applicable as a governance principle for SoNash's project vs. user memory distinction. No current rules prevent project-specific observations from staying project-scoped.
Phase-based rule loading achieves approximately 70% token reduction according to the cursor-memory-bank implementation. This pattern is adoptable without installing cursor-memory-bank.

SoNash already implicitly operates as a multi-layer memory system: CLAUDE.md (always-inject) to MEMORY.md index (session-start inject) to JSONL state files (audit trail) to mcp__memory graph (on-demand retrieval). The layers exist; explicit tier boundaries and promotion rules do not.
context-sync (Intina47) is local-only. Its SQLite database has no built-in cross-machine push/pull mechanism. Wave 1 research description of it as cross-machine was incorrect.

OneDrive creates a duplicate file with a machine name suffix on concurrent writes to plain markdown files. For a solo operator who never runs both machines simultaneously, this is detectable and recoverable — not silent data corruption.
Pointing autoMemoryDirectory at a git-tracked directory has no publicly documented community implementation. It is the highest-signal sync finding but is untested in production.
doobidoo/mcp-memory-service has a Cloudflare Workers backend enabling true cross-locale sync via remote MCP. It is a Phase 2 option. Docker is required for local install on Windows without admin.

memoir requires npm install -g. This needs no admin if Node.js was installed with nvm-windows, but requires admin with a system-level Node.js install. The work locale's Node.js install method is unconfirmed.

The three highest-priority architectural gaps: (1) canonical-memory divergence + autoMemoryDirectory activation; (2) session memory consolidation pipeline; (3) memory decay metadata for 39 individual files. All three addressable without new infrastructure.

Confidence decay rates across cognitive memory types are consistent across OpenMemory and yuvalsuede implementations: emotional/progress context is most volatile; reflective/architectural content is least volatile.
The supermemory cloud plugin has an active Windows stdin bug (GitHub Issue #25, open as of February 2026). It is not recommended for this environment until confirmed fixed.
Engram is a single Go binary with MIT license, explicit Claude Code plugin marketplace support, and SQLite+FTS5. No Windows compatibility issues documented. No admin or Docker required.

The community dream-skill plugin replicates AutoDream's 4-phase consolidation cycle as a manually-triggered skill. It is available now and is the most accessible implementation of session memory consolidation.

claude-mem's PostToolUse hooks broke for 2+ months (November 2025 through January 2026) when Claude Code changed its runtime internals. This demonstrates the hook maintenance burden for third-party hook-dependent systems.
OneDrive availability at the work locale (jbell) is unconfirmed. If unavailable, the recommended sync alternative is git-only (canonical-memory + autoMemoryDirectory) or Syncthing portable binary.

mcp-memory-keeper is confirmed Windows-compatible via npx with no admin required. Its git branch-derived topic channels align with SoNash's multi-branch workflow. The minimal profile should be used to reduce token overhead.
The existing system is 70-80% of the way to the clean-slate architecture ideal, with the three roles (GOVERN/KNOW/RECALL) already implemented as CLAUDE.md/MEMORY.md/JSONL state files.
The hybrid approach (D8a) and clean-slate design (D9a) are not competing answers — D9a is the design target and D8a is the incremental route to reach it.
Setting autoMemoryDirectory to point at .claude/canonical-memory/ (git-tracked) is the highest-signal single action for cross-locale sync, requiring zero new infrastructure.

The canonical-memory directory is currently diverged: missing approximately 7 feedback entries and incorrectly describing user expertise as 'Node.js expert' rather than 'non-developer director.' Reconciliation is required before autoMemoryDirectory is configured.

AutoDream is confirmed LIVE on this account (tengu_onyx_plover flag). Research findings that describe it as server-gated are superseded by user confirmation. Testing AutoDream behavior for 3-5 sessions before adding dream-skill is required to avoid conflict.

4 of 14 current mechanisms have drifted from their intended function, giving a ~29% base-rate probability that any new mechanism will be abandoned within 20 sessions unless it is zero-maintenance or fully automated.

The session-end pipeline is the load-bearing ritual: if it is skipped, SESSION_CONTEXT, TDMS consolidation, learning system updates, and proposed enhancements all fail. It must not be lengthened beyond what can be executed in 2-3 minutes.

The MCP memory startup hang risk (5-6 minutes, closed NOT_PLANNED by Anthropic) is permanent. The mitigation — never add 'check MCP memory at session start' to CLAUDE.md or any skill — must remain in place indefinitely.

Chroma (npm chromadb) is ARM64-only on Windows x64 and cannot be used as a vector store in this environment. This is documented in claude-mem Issue #1146 and applies to any tool using the npm chromadb package.

sqlite-vec is the best-fit vector store for this environment — embedded, no server process, installs via npm, v0.1.9 confirmed released 2026-03-31. Critical gap: npm package behavior on Windows x64 without admin has not been independently verified in this specific environment.
The existing mcp__memory knowledge graph is adequate for current scale (~500-1,500 entities). Enhancement: set MEMORY_FILE_PATH to a git-tracked path so the graph syncs cross-locale via git.
Neo4j is eliminated as a knowledge graph option — requires admin (Windows installer) or Docker (also admin). Hard block for this environment regardless of capability.

Local ONNX embeddings via @huggingface/transformers + all-MiniLM-L6-v2 are the correct primary embedding choice: zero cost, full privacy, offline, no admin. Known gap: Node.js v22 compatibility not independently verified.
Voyage AI is rejected despite Anthropic's endorsement: default retains training data, opt-out requires admin dashboard access, and cost is 3x OpenAI's backup option.

SQLite via cloud sync (OneDrive, Dropbox) carries silent corruption risk. SQLite official docs explicitly state network filesystem use is unsupported. Keep SQLite stores (L3 episodic layer) locale-local only.

The content-type-prior filter (A-MAC paper) eliminates approximately 70% of low-signal memory write candidates and is the single highest-leverage memory quality improvement available at near-zero cost.

Adding a Memory Admission Policy whitelist to CLAUDE.md is the highest ROI per hour of any pattern in the research — 45 minutes, zero infrastructure, immediate quality improvement on every future session.
The existing feedback_*.md file convention is already an implicit content-type-prior implementation. Making it explicit and extending it to all memory categories is formalization, not invention.

Confidence decay (TTL) must NEVER be applied to behavioral corrections (feedback_*.md) or architectural decisions. The failure mode — re-teaching corrected behaviors because they were decayed — is worse than the memory entropy the decay was meant to solve.

Restructuring MEMORY.md as a token-aware progressive disclosure index (adding ~tokens, type, salience columns) reduces unnecessary topic file loading and improves session startup efficiency. Takes 2-3 hours, zero new infrastructure.
Adding the episodic_memory_show permission to the allow-list is a 15-minute, zero-risk enhancement that completes the episodic retrieval path from search-only to search+retrieve.

codebase-memory-mcp fills a structural code intelligence gap that no session memory tool addresses. Windows amd64 binary, auto-detect install, near-zero ongoing maintenance. Complementary to session memory, not competing.

Engram (Go binary, FTS5, MCP stdio, Bare MCP mode) is the best standalone explicit knowledge store for this stack — aligns with the Go tool pattern already deployed, no shell dependencies in Bare MCP mode, export/import for cross-machine sync.

claude-mem must not be installed as a primary framework: 5 documented Windows-specific failures, CLAUDE.md subdirectory pollution bug closed NOT_PLANNED, AGPL-3.0 license, Bun+ChromaDB+PowerShell dependency chain the no-admin work locale cannot satisfy.

The autoMemoryDirectory setting was shipped in Claude Code v2.1.74 (March 12, 2026) specifically to support redirecting memory writes to git-tracked or cloud-synced paths. It is the official mechanism for cross-locale memory sync.
autoMemoryDirectory cannot be set in project settings (.claude/settings.json, git-tracked). It must be manually set in settings.local.json at each locale. This is a security restriction, not a bug.

The Supermemory cloud plugin ($19/month) carries a Windows stdin hang (Issue #25, open since Feb 2026) and transmits full session transcripts to a third party. Both are disqualifying for this environment.
OMEGA Memory's 95.4% LongMemEval performance is irrelevant because it requires WSL2, which requires admin access unavailable at the work locale. Hard block regardless of quality.

The existing .claude/state/consolidation.json + run-consolidation.js pattern is exactly the infrastructure needed for a memory consolidation pipeline. The gap is not 'build a new system' but 'generalize the existing pattern from PR reviews to all memory types.'

The existing MEMORY.md taxonomy (user_*, feedback_*, project_*, reference_*) maps directly to the A-MAC content-type-prior categories. The admission filter is already implicitly implemented; it needs to be made explicit and enforced.
Context rot from injecting too many irrelevant memories is a confirmed risk. Distractors compound non-linearly: a single stale memory entry actively degrades output quality, not just wastes tokens.

Current startup token budget (~12,300-12,600 tokens estimated) is within safe bounds. A startup budget guard in session-begin should warn at >18,000 tokens — the threshold where degradation becomes measurable.

The LongMemEval benchmark shows approximately 30% accuracy drop on memorizing information across sustained interactions even for commercial assistants. Conservative retrieval design (fewer, higher-confidence memories) is warranted.

Consolidation that runs in context-free mode (AutoDream's architecture) risks compressing nuanced conditional rules into flat rules. Archive originals; never delete source memories during consolidation.

The 'minimum viable custom' principle applies: build only what Anthropic demonstrably will not ship. AutoDream is live, native cross-device sync is in the roadmap. Building infrastructure Anthropic ships in 6 months is wasted effort.

With 25 hooks already running, any new PostToolUse hook doing non-trivial work must use fire-and-forget queue architecture. The 30-second hook timeout is a hard constraint. A performance budget for new hooks is required before adding any.

The clean-slate architecture's primary gaps versus the current system are: (a) no admission gate at write time, (b) no decay/TTL enforcement, (c) consolidation not generalized beyond PR reviews, (d) canonical-memory divergence. All four are addressable without replacing any existing mechanism.

Reasoning trace capture (storing how Claude reasoned, not just what it decided) is supported by Reflexion (NeurIPS 2023) showing +11% HumanEval improvement from re-reading past reasoning traces. A lightweight reasoning-traces.jsonl implements this without cipher's full infrastructure.

Memory promotion rules (project-scoped knowledge elevating to global scope only after 3+ session recurrences) would prevent project-specific noise from polluting global memory. The existing MEMORY.md taxonomy already has the scope structure; the rule formalizes it.

Phase-based rule loading via .claude/rules/ glob-scoped files can reduce CLAUDE.md effective token load from ~4,800 to ~3,500 tokens for sessions where phase-specific sections are irrelevant. The .claude/rules/ directory already supports this.
T2-D (completing autoMemoryDirectory at the home locale) requires physical access to the home machine and cannot be done remotely. It should be scheduled as a dedicated session at that locale.

The autoMemoryDirectory + git-tracked directory pattern appears to be novel — the research found no reference implementations of this specific combination. Treat Phase 1 as an experiment with a defined rollback path.

STATE_SCHEMA.md documents 10 state files while the actual state directory has 82 files — 8x drift. Fixing this is a T1 free win: one session, 20-30 minutes, prevents wasted orientation time in every future session.

Docker-based memory tools (Hindsight, OpenMemory/Mem0, Qdrant standalone, Weaviate) are eliminated for this environment regardless of quality ratings. Docker requires Hyper-V or WSL2; no admin at work locale is a hard block.
